Rules-Game Settings:
- Must be in the Discord.

- Timer: 99 seconds

- Rounds: 3

- Hazards: OFF

- Assists: ON

- Matches Format: Initially, all matches are Best of 3 (FT2). As we progress to Winners Finals, Losers Finals, and Grand Finals, the format shifts to Best of 5 (FT3).

- Reselection: Losers are allowed to switch Character, Assist, and Map; winners must stick with their choices.

- "North America Only": Even though it says North America Only, other players that are not from North America can join and participate in the tournament. - Game Ownership: It is recommended that you own JoJo's All-Star Battle R; however, all you really need is Parsec.

- Bans: Any glitches resulting in a softlock are forbidden. In the event of encountering such glitches, players must replay the match.

Rules-Glitches:
This is a rare but known bug which can occur with toss-ups on certain characters, most notably when Steve Miller is trying to catch the disc. These points are honored in tournament as the bug is very hard to reproduce and there is no easy way to discount these points without completely disrupting the match.

In the event that it happens, restart the match and try to replicate the score before the glitch. If this cannot be done, contact the TO/Bracket Runners.

- All Matches must provide either a fightcade replay or a video replay in extreme cases. If no replay can be provided, Staff reserves the right to choose a player to advance.
- In the event that a match cannot be played or a lobby cannot be formed between players, inform the TO.

Chen-Mao players must be aware of the desync issues she can cause if she is selected second on character select screen, and are responsible for resetting the game themselves to avoid this. As such, any would-be Chen-Mao players must be player 1 and manage resetting the game when necessary. 
- To be player 1 on Fightcade, you must be the one to challenge your opponent.
- Any desyncs caused by Chen-Mao will grant the game to their opponent.
- If a player forgets to reset the game upon a loss and picks Chen-Mao for the next match, the game must be reset before the match starts (FIGHT! appears on screen). Resetting the emulator once the match has started will grant the game to the Chen-Mao player's opponent.

- These rules do not apply in case of a Chen-Mao mirror.

Nanami is banned to avoid desyncs.
